creator: mutoh@openedu.org
text:
Hi, I'm Takeshi MUTOH.

Thank you for your check my update.

On 2012/05/10, at 11:29, scheidell@FreeBSD.org wrote:
> Synopsis: Maintainer Update japanese/seaside to 080908-sq39
>=20
> State-Changed-From-To: open->feedback
> State-Changed-By: scheidell
> State-Changed-When: Thu May 10 02:27:45 UTC 2012
> State-Changed-Why:=20
> Found an issue during package building in tinderbox.
> Can you check?

In my ports/167312 patch, I change directory to install data from =
${PREFIX}/share/squeak/ to ${PREFIX}/lib/squeak/ at Makefile and =
pkg-descr.=20
So I think no problem related the place of data.

> tail of build log:
> =
<http://lorie.secnap.net/tb/index.php?action=3Ddisplay_markup_log&build=3D=
7-scheidell_I386&id=3D1799#587>
> pkg_add squeak-4.4.7.tbz
>=20
> ------------------------- Please note: -------------------------
>=20
>  In order to be able to make use of squeak you need to have an
>    image and a changes file in your working directory as well
>    as access to a source file. This is easy by using inisqueak.
>    Please see the inisqueak(1) manpage for details.
>=20
>  Make sure you have the following values in your environment
>    variables:
>    PATH:            /usr/local/bin
>    LD_LIBRARY_PATH: /usr/local/lib
>                     /usr/local/lib/squeak/4.4.7-2375
>=20
> ------------------ Thanks for your attention! ------------------
>=20
> =3D=3D=3D>  Installing for ja-seaside-070930.s38_1
> =3D=3D=3D>   ja-seaside-070930.s38_1 depends on executable: squeak - =
found
> =3D=3D=3D>   Generating temporary packing list
> =3D=3D=3D>  Checking if japanese/seaside already installed
> (cd /work/a/ports/japanese/seaside/work && install  -o root -g wheel =
-m 444 Seaside-JPatched20070930-sq38.image.gz=20
> /usr/local/share/squeak/)
> install: /usr/local/share/squeak/: No such file or directory
> *** Error code 71

I think this log shows not new version 080908.s39 but current ports =
version 070930-sq38.
So please check state of build port tree, and build again.

See also my redports log:
- http://redports.org/buildarchive/20120511041528-85608/

Thanks in advance
--
Takeshi MUTOH <mutoh@openedu.org>
